title: ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM - 211info
text: ADELANTE MUJERES ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic education program for Latina women working towards a GED, including coursework in English Language Development (ELD), mathematics, grammar, parenting, and an 18-week seminar focused on conflict resolution (ESPERE). The Early Childhood Education program provides preschool and child care for participants' children. 2030 Main Street Suite A Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Low-income Spanish-speaking women and their children, ages 6 weeks-5 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Classes are held 5 days per week for a full year Email (503) 992-0078 . - . . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 992-0078 Ma. Luisa Piñon or Yasi Lemus-isla [email protected] OR [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for appointment FEES: Varies by service, but fees are minimal DOCUMENTS: Proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Washington County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: TriMet bus 57 to Forest Grove, Main Street stop Services: Adult Basic Education * Hispanic/Latino Community English as a Second Language * Hispanic/Latino Community GED/High School Equivalency Test Instruction * Hispanic/Latino Community Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND FINANCE - MICROENTERPRISE PROGRAMS SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - CHICAS YOUTH DEVELOPMENT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. ADELANTE MUJERES
